name: Tessa Molyneaux
ai_summary: Tessa Molyneaux is a pre-nursing student with extensive babysitting experience and volunteering at Primary Children's Hospital emergency department. She is located in Salt Lake City with 30-mile travel capability and seeks full-time hours.

transcripts.q3: My name is Tessa. I am currently a student at the U and I am pre-nursing applying to nursing school in a year. And I am pursuing a pediatric, clinical research minor. I have always grown up watching my little siblings and babysitting for neighbors and family friends. Doing lots of babysitting in my ears. And I also Have volunteered at permanent Primary Children's, in the emergency department working with children in their most vulnerable state. I love children. I think they bring so much joy to life and helped me have a better perspective on life and I think it's just fun to give back to like place that I once was in my life.
transcripts.q4: I currently live in Salt Lake City Utah but in two weeks I am moving to Payson Utah and I am willing to travel up to 30 minutes.
transcripts.q5: I'm looking for something. Not quite that meets the full time hours but not minimal hours, somewhere between 25 and 30 hours, probably some more closer to the full-time ends but and willing to work any amount of shift Any day of the week, my schedule is really free.
transcripts.q6: What's conflict between tell to children? I feel it is important to make sure both children feel seen. So it feels like not one. Child is getting chosen over the other but also to Help them know that they were in the wrong, without making them feel bad about things. So I usually talk to both children. Get both sides, figure out what's going on. And gently remind the children, that what they may be like, weren't doing was okay. Or if they're having a hard time sharing to help them. Figure out ways that sharing works for them just honestly the best way is just to talk through both talk through it with both children and figure out what works best for them.
transcripts.q7: I will make a good teacher because I am patient and I'm kind, but I'm also still young and still love to do activities with children and make sure that kids are still having a good time. I think a lot of time these days, especially with like internet and tablets and stuff children are super focused on not Like things that are more modern and I think playing and Imagination is super important and I also think education is super important as I'm in college. So I will help children be able to get a good kick, start on their education early in life.
